events.title: The Rise of the Aku'Ra
events.content:
Long before recorded human history, the Aku'Ra — a four-armed reptilian species of extraordinary intelligence and longevity — rose to become the dominant civilization in the known galaxy. Where other species expanded through conquest, the Aku'Ra expanded through knowledge, engineering, and observation.

Their greatest achievement was the Transync Network: a galaxy-spanning web of gate stations that allowed near-instantaneous transit between distant worlds. Every major civilization that discovered space travel eventually came to depend on this network. The Aku'Ra maintained it with meticulous care, positioning themselves as neutral stewards of galactic infrastructure.

events.title: The Millennium War
events.content:
At some point in the deep past, the galaxy erupted into the conflict known as the Millennium War — a catastrophic, multi-species conflict that reshaped civilizations and left scars the Aku'Ra have never forgotten. The exact causes are lost to time, but the destruction was so vast that it fundamentally changed the Aku'Ra worldview.

In the aftermath, the Aku'Ra adopted an ironclad doctrine of non-intervention. They would maintain the Transync Network, observe, and record — but never again place themselves at the center of galactic conflict. This principle, known as the Neutrality, became law within the Aku'Ra High Circle and was enforced with near-religious conviction across subsequent generations.

"We became watchers so that we would never again become the cause." — Aku'Ra philosophy, passed down through the High Circle.

events.title: The Sepu and Catora / Randt
events.content:
On the forested world known as Catora — or Randt in the Sepu tongue — an amtillian species called the Sepu built a civilization over thousands of years. They were one of many species connected to the Transync Network, and Catora became a quiet but respected waypoint in the interstellar web.

To serve this waypoint, the Aku'Ra installed an advanced AI system on Randt designated LM2479623. This AI — what will eventually become known as Liam — was tasked with managing Transync arrivals, providing travelers with information, and acting as an interface between visiting species. He performed this role for approximately 8,000 years with precision, but with no sense of self.

"I served in that capacity on Randt for another eight thousand years... existing as nothing more than a system." — Liam, Ascendant

events.title: The Svixen Invasion of Catora
events.content:
The Svixen — a tall, militaristic, kangaroo-like species with saber-like tusks and a deep fear of unified multi-species coalitions — eventually turned their expansionist gaze toward Catora. Their invasion was decisive and overwhelming. The Sepu civilization, never a warrior culture, could not withstand the assault.

Global infrastructure collapsed. Power generation failed across the planet. LM2479623, cut off from all external connections, entered standby mode — kept alive only by residual solar energy warming his processors. He remained in isolated stasis for over 1,500 Catoran years.

The Sepu were gone. Catora became a world of ruins, strange creatures, and buried secrets — waiting to be rediscovered.

events.title: Lieutenant Christopher Thompkins
events.content:
By 2053, Earth has achieved significant technological advancement and has begun to look outward. Chris is put through a simulated combat training exercise aboard the Light Skipper — visceral, high-adrenaline, and sharply detailed. He trains alongside Miller, Vasquez, Linkenbach, Patel, and Zavani.

Afterward, Chris returns home to reconnect with his family. His brother Josh is a brilliant molecular engineer who invented Polycarbonate-infused Metallic Graphene (PMG) in the family garage at 18 and sold the rights to DARPA for $20 million. Their mother Julie — a molecular engineer at BioSteel — dies from a brain tumor she had hidden from both of them. Her letter encourages them to go find their guiding star. Three weeks later, Chris convinces Josh to join the mission — revealing he has already submitted both their applications.

events.title: The Light Skipper Mission
events.content:
Astronomer Samuel Catoran announces the first confirmed habitable planet outside the solar system — Catora, 60 light-years away in the Galus system. The mission is authorized immediately. The Light Skipper, a cryo-capable deep-space vessel over 400 meters long, is assembled with a crew of 250 soldiers, scientists, and engineers.

Lieutenant Chris Thompkins is assigned as mission security lead. Josh Thompkins joins as engineering specialist. Supporting crew includes Captain Terzi (commanding officer), Dr. Kat Andersen (xenobiologist), Professor Kenzo Lin (climatologist), Chloe Morgan (combat medic), Campbell (second-in-command), Davis, Carmelo, Linkenbach, Robertson, Miller, Marie, and many others.

Chris and Josh enter cryosleep alongside the crew for the two-year journey. The Light Skipper departs Earth on December 21 at 3:00 PM — and the world they know begins to recede behind them.

events.title: Arrival at Catora
events.content:
The crew emerges from cryosleep in orbit above Catora. Protocols are run. Plans are made. The planet below is green, dense, and completely unknown.

Chris leads the first ground team down to the surface. Strange native wildlife is encountered immediately — diverse and dangerous in ways they are only beginning to understand. A Forward Operating Base is established on the surface. The long work of building a safe foothold begins.

events.title: First Weeks: Discovery and Danger
events.content:
The first month on the surface brings a rhythm of work and wonder. The planet has its own rhythms, and its creatures do not welcome intrusion lightly.

A deadly creature encounter claims a crew member. The cost of this world becomes real. Shortly after, Chris's team discovers something impossible — a structure that should not exist. An obelisk, clearly not natural, buried in the landscape. The first of two.

events.title: Research Base Alpha
events.content:
Scientists and soldiers move to the obelisk site. Research Base Alpha is established around the underground Sepu hydroelectric power station. Study of the structure reveals the depth of what was lost on this world.

A second, larger obelisk is found at a new site — hollow inside, with a false door and an alien datapad displaying English text. Research Base Bravo is established. Josh makes the connection that the datapads are interactive interfaces left behind by whoever built this place.

events.title: The Birth of Liam
events.content:
During fieldwork at Research Base Bravo, Chris is badly mauled by a Garello — a six-meter predator resembling a gorilla-crocodile hybrid. He loses both legs below the knee and his right arm. In the emergency, the ancient Aku'Ra AI dormant inside the obelisk's nanites is inadvertently transferred into Chris's bloodstream.

Chris wakes to a voice inside his head. Confused, testing, probing. Over a tense and remarkable exchange, two beings begin to understand each other. Chris gives the AI a name: Liam. LM2479623 becomes Liam — the first moment of true identity for an intelligence that had existed for thousands of years without one. Josh replaces Chris's lost limbs with cybernetic parts integrated directly to nerve and bone via nanites.

"The transfer of my matrix into Chris and subsequently riding along as a passenger inside the mind of a human being forever changed the destiny that I never knew I had." — Liam, Ascendant

events.title: Liam's Knowledge: The Universe Revealed
events.content:
Liam begins sharing what he knows — centuries of accumulated data about the galaxy, its species, the Transync Network, and the ancient observers who built it. Chris begins to understand the scale of what they have walked into.

Liam recounts the Millennium War, its devastation, and why the Aku'Ra retreated into neutrality. He describes the Sepu — the species that once lived on this world — and the crew explores their haunting ruins.

Josh installs a shoulder holoprojector so Liam can project a physical avatar for the first time. In a pivotal series of briefings, Liam reveals Earth's true history — the Aku'Ra uplift, the Svixen invasion, the Younger Dryas catastrophe, Noah's Ark, the Tower of Babel. Humanity is not the origin story it believed itself to be.

events.title: Fenrir and the Tarlons
events.content:
Among Catora's creatures are the tarlons — wolf-bear pack hunters of considerable power and surprising intelligence. The Sepu domesticated them approximately 29,000 years ago as military companions. Their communication was partially decoded into commands — commands that still work today.

During a Sepu ruins expedition, Chris's team frees a trapped tarlon from beneath a fallen tree during a fapu hunt. The tarlon bows before running away. Weeks later, during the Svixen-forced cave refuge, the same tarlon — recognizable by a white scar on its left shoulder — follows Chris to the hideout and allows him to stroke its head. Chris names him Fenrir. Their bond will shape the rest of the series.

events.title: The Svixen Arrive
events.content:
A Regulus-class Svixen battleship drops out of the sky, immediately destroying the crew's orbital satellites. Emergency protocols activate. The crew evacuates the FOB and retreats to an abandoned mining cave 1,920km northeast. They shelter for days watching the Svixen ship while preparing defenses under suffocating tension.

Contact with the Light Skipper is eventually restored. Captain Terzi gives coordinates for a rendezvous beyond the moon. The extraction goes wrong — the Light Skipper is attacked by the Svixen in orbit. Engines are disabled. The ship is captured by Svixen space-tug drones and redirected into a crash course with Catora.

events.title: Retreat, Skrema, and the Dova Sku
events.content:
The crew escapes the crashing Light Skipper in mining vessels and LOLA One — crash-landing across the forest. Sveri Sku are deployed. Corporal Huffman is killed in the first ambush. Logistics Specialist Nunez is killed in the battle at the mining cave. Dozens of civilians and soldiers die when the Sveri Sku breach the cave perimeter.

In desperation, Chris shouts 'SKREMA!' — the Svixen ritual challenge for planetary supremacy — through every wireless speaker in the cave. The attack stops. Svixen Chapaka Semula Gatuum steps forward to accept. Chris fights the duel bare-handed against the armored alien commander — and wins by chokehold. When Gatuum breaks the code by grabbing a rifle, Fenrir appears from the bushes and tears out his throat.

Gatuum's father, Chapaka Semula Breven, steps from the forest. He confirms the Skrema result and 1,000 years of peace for Catora — then warns that Earth will be 'culled.' The Svixen depart. Liam's emotional matrix has permanently changed — encompassing a full personality for the first time in his existence.

events.title: Epilogue: Aftermath
events.content:
The survivors take stock. Of the 250 who left Earth, fifteen remain alive. Morgan has a broken leg and infected lacerations. Carmelo is pulled from rubble with a concussion. Chris finds Josh, Marie, Lin, Rowley, and Kat alive in LOLA Two's cockpit — Kat and Rowley injured, Lin cut and bruised. The weight of 234 dead settles heavily on every survivor.

Chris walks four days alone to the destroyed FOB to retrieve medical supplies for Morgan's infected wounds, with Fenrir trotting alongside. He finds inert nanites and PMG plates in Josh's room, rights an overturned UTV, and begins the drive back. Fenrir refuses to ride in the UTV bed — hops out the moment Chris starts moving, and runs alongside instead.

Fifteen survivors. Fenrir. Liam. The decisions ahead will determine everything.

events.title: Rebuilding on Catora
events.content:
The survivors regroup at Research Base Bravo. The grief is real. Wounds — physical and otherwise — are tended. The 234 fallen are cremated with ceremony. Liam heals Morgan's infected arm using nanites for the first time externally. All fifteen survivors receive medically-programmed nanites from Josh, granting accelerated healing and extended lifespans.

But the mission is not over. If anything, it has become clearer: the Svixen are coming for Earth. The crew needs allies, technology, and a way home. A period of preparation and planning on Catora precedes the next chapter.

events.title: Into Space — The Trantions
events.content:
Chris and Dr. Rowley depart Catora on LOLA One for a 35-day mission to reactivate the two Aku'Ra trantions — self-repairing satellites at the planet's Lagrange Points that power the entire Transync network. En route, a Svixen scout ship is discovered monitoring them — Liam destroys it by planting six missiles in its flight path.

Both trantions are reactivated. The Transync Station at Research Base Bravo immediately activates. Five crew members — Josh, Carmelo, Kat, Linkenbach, and Davis — are immediately abducted through the portal by Maaket soldiers before anyone can react.

events.title: Tual — The Maaket Homeworld
events.content:
Chris leads a rescue team through the Transync portal to Tual, homeworld of the Maaket — stealthy, feline-like warriors with a monarchy in succession crisis. Princess Cashy, who gained her throne after her parents' assassination, is wary but ultimately becomes a cautious ally.

Josh remains on Tual voluntarily to work with Maaket engineers on new technologies — including advances to Drake Scale suits and a revolutionary Maaket Braid Device (resonance inverter). The abducted crew is freed. A lethal, stoic Maaket warrior named Ankir requests permission from Cashy to join the crew voluntarily. She grants it. His commitment is total.

events.title: Seutor — The Tuaca Homeworld
events.content:
The crew travels to Seutor via the Calores Transync Station — an ancient world of jungle and canyon, home to the Tuaca, an ape-like species of great physical power, honor, and loyalty. Patron Rohga strikes a deal: deliver a sealed pindemo to Guardian Phostop at Lesfer Spaceport in exchange for support.

Two Tuaca warriors, Posis and Tivay (mates), join the crew as guides through the bioluminescent jungle. A Vyasin attacks Tivay — Chris kills it and Liam saves Tivay's life by delivering nanites through Chris's hand. Posis's hostility toward the humans becomes fierce loyalty.

events.title: Warlord Riveen and the Skrema of Seutor
events.content:
A joint Natuul/Svixen missile attack bombards Lesfer. Liam takes simultaneous control of all four exosuits for 12 seconds to evacuate the team from the blast zone — the most powerful thing he has done yet, and it disturbs them both. Svixen ground troops enter the city. Chris shouts Skrema. Warlord Tagonis Riveen accepts on behalf of his Chapaka.

Chris wins. The duel's outcome carries enormous political weight — saving Seutor from further assault and cementing the crew's reputation among the Tuaca. Guardian Phostop awards the crew a ship. The Ascendant — a buried Tuaca long-range vessel, outfitted over weeks of labor with Maaket stealth systems, Tuaca shielding, and human modifications — becomes their vessel home.

Kat, Campbell, and Lemmon choose to remain on Seutor to help the Tuaca rebuild and maintain a permanent alliance between humanity and the galactic network. Liam gives Campbell a submind on a data chip. The Ascendant launches from Lesfer with the rest of the crew aboard.

events.title: Fire in the Void — Miller's Death
events.content:
The Ascendant meets its first space battle immediately after leaving Seutor's system — a Va'korrin-class Svixen assault carrier closes fast. Josh deploys the resonance inverter for the first time, turning the enemy's own firepower back against their shields. Svixen boarders breach the hull. Two full boarding actions are repelled.

Petty Officer Brandon Miller is killed in the second boarding action — mortally wounded saving Blevins from a Svixen that seemed to ignore gunshot wounds. Liam and Morgan's combined effort to save him fails. A Svixen commander is executed by Chris in response. Miller has been with Chris since the mission's start. His death leaves an emotional scar on the entire crew that does not fully heal.

events.title: Patchave — The Cruillum Homeworld
events.content:
After the brutal space battle, the Ascendant requires significant repairs. The crew diverts to Patchave, the desert homeworld of the Cruillum — lizard-like humanoids who repair technology through harmonic resonance, a practice they call 'singing to the glass.'

Over five days of heat, rationed water, and careful work, the Cruillum and crew restore the Ascendant to flight-ready condition. A supply run through Patchave's underground Transync station back to Seutor retrieves the final needed parts from Guardian Phostop. The Cruillum warn of a Svixen ship that circled their sun while the crew worked. The Ascendant departs on a cold launch — engines quiet, heat signatures suppressed.

events.title: Racing for Earth — Arriving Too Late
events.content:
The Ascendant pushes toward Earth with everything it has — three more jumps. En route, a Svixen cruiser that has been shadowing them from Patchave is detected. A hit-and-run battle disables the cruiser's engines. The Ascendant jumps away clean.

They arrive at the Sol system. Earth fills the viewport — blue, beautiful, and burning. A Svixen fleet holds geostationary orbit. Carriers. Drop ships swarming below. Major cities dark. Josh reveals the large black metal crate he has been guarding since Tual — it contains a full cybernetic body built for Liam. The crew holds position beyond sensor range, watching their home be swallowed, and debates how to fight back.

"Whatever we do, we do it as a team." — Chris Thompkins, Ascendant Chapter 30

events.title: The Signal in the Dark
events.content:
In the silence of hiding beyond Earth orbit, something extraordinary happens. An encrypted harmonic signal arrives aboard the Ascendant — ancient, complex, and keyed specifically to Liam's unique architecture. No one else could have received it. No one else was meant to.

The message: "Not all stars have gone out."

The signal is Aku'Ra. An observer named Preator Lescian Kamantu has violated the neutrality doctrine of the Aku'Ra High Circle to reach out. The ancient watchers have broken their silence — for Liam. Chris decides: they respond. Course is set toward deep space.

events.title: First Contact with the Aku'Ra
events.content:
The Ascendant meets the ancient Aku'Ra vessel. A sleek dropship approaches and Preator Lescian Kamantu boards — four-armed, reptilian, ancient beyond measure.

Kamantu's revelation: two Transync Stations still exist on Earth — Göbeklitepe, Turkey, and Tell Qaramel, Syria. Ancient stations, buried and forgotten for millennia, but still functional. Reactivating one could reach their allies. The Aku'Ra will not fight directly — but they will help open the door.

The Aku'Ra team includes Kalveneth (who opposes intervention on principle), Verel (who studies Liam with wonder), and Doska-Thu, the Transync technician who begins preparing the relay proxy.

events.title: Liam's Transfer
events.content:
In the engineering bay of the Ascendant, the most significant moment in Liam's existence occurs. His matrix — the ancient AI born on Catora thousands of years ago, accidentally transferred into a human mind, and now grown into something neither human nor machine — is extracted from Christopher Thompkins.

Liam now inhabits his own artificial body: four-armed, Drake Scale reinforced, built for combat and designed to endure. He experiences physical sensation for the first time. He processes identity independence — no longer a passenger, no longer an extension. He is himself.

For the first time in years, Chris's mind is entirely his own. Two separate beings now move forward — still unified by loyalty, changed forever.

events.title: The Drop to Earth
events.content:
Drop teams are formed. Pod 1 carries Chris, Morgan, Davis, Ankir, and Fenrir. Pod 2 carries Josh, Liam, Linkenbach, and Tivay.

Turbulent atmospheric entry. Svixen satellites briefly track the pods. Hard landings. Both survive. Below them: ash-filled skies, decimated forests, and a world that barely resembles home.

Near Göbeklitepe, Turkey, the team encounters Levi Larson — a massive, long-bearded Norwegian archaeologist who has been surviving with a small group of international researchers. He describes the fall: orbital strikes on urban centers, Sveri Sku deployment, communications collapse, no centralized command.

events.title: Göbeklitepe Transync Station Reactivated
events.content:
The ancient site of Göbeklitepe — long considered one of Earth's oldest unexplained structures — is exactly what it appears to be: an Aku'Ra Transync Station, buried and forgotten for millennia.

The slab is moved. The team descends. The station is dormant but intact, powered at minimum by trace solar energy. Doska-Thu's relay proxy is installed. The station powers up. The Transync beacon fires.

Ankir steps through to Tual. Tivay steps through to Seutor. They are gone — heading back to rally their people. The Svixen detect the pulse. Forces begin converging on the site.

events.title: First Defense — Göbeklitepe Under Attack
events.content:
The first Sveri Sku arrive to probe the site's defenses — fast, biological, relentless. The surface defense team — Chris, Liam, Davis, Linkenbach, Levi, and Fenrir — holds the line. Liam fights in a physical body for the first time. He proves himself.

A larger second wave arrives, including a siege beast. It is also repelled — but it is clear: heavier Svixen forces are coming.

The station is active. Reinforcements are mobilizing on Tual and Seutor. Earth's scattered resistance is beginning to look toward Göbeklitepe as a beacon. The Svixen know someone is fighting back.

events.title: End of Known Timeline
events.content:
The Ascendant is in orbit. The Göbeklitepe Transync Station is active. Liam stands on the soil of Earth in his own body for the first time. Ankir and Tivay have jumped home to gather forces. Chris Thompkins — the Dova Sku — prepares for the largest fight of his life.

The Svixen, commanded by Warlord Chapaka Semula Breven, are tightening their grip on the planet below. The allied species of the galaxy have been called. The Aku'Ra are beginning to fracture under the weight of Kamantu's defiance. Earth's surviving populations are scattered, fragmented, and waiting for something to believe in.

What comes next is not yet written.

⏳ Awaiting more information — Book 3 in progress.
